pr.title: lb: improve fn_80027488 matching, tidy a few header signatures
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Improved the fuzzy match for lb audio function fn_80027488 by referencing standalone extern arrays at known symbol addresses instead of struct-field aliases, and cleaned several lb headers by replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders with explicit void signatures already used by the C files. No function reached 100%, but decomp.dev reported fn_80027488 improving from 89.55% to 94.86%, with build verification green.

key_files.role: Changed fn_80027488 locals from lbl_80433710.x194/x274 struct-field accesses to direct lbl_804338A4/lbl_80433984 extern array references.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with explicit void signatures for lb_8000FA94 and lb_80014534.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with explicit void signatures for lb_8001955C, fn_800195FC, lb_800198E0, and lb_80019900.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with explicit void signatures for lb_8001C600, lb_8001C658, lb_8001CF18, and lb_8001D1F4.

what_changed: In fn_80027488, changed int* a = lbl_80433710.x194 and int* b = lbl_80433710.x274 to direct extern references int* b = lbl_80433984 and int* a = lbl_804338A4.
Declared b before a in fn_80027488 so MWCC materializes the two addresses in the order expected by the original code.
Tidied lb_00F9.h, lb_0192.h, and lbcardgame.h by replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholder prototypes with concrete void return/parameter signatures.
Header changes were described as compile-time no-ops because the macros already expand to void, but they make the ABI clearer and align headers with existing C definitions.
decomp.dev reported one unmatched-item improvement: main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax fn_80027488 improved from 89.55% to 94.86% fuzzy match.
smart_moves: Used symbol-address knowledge to avoid treating lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984 as fields of lbl_80433710 when direct extern arrays better reflect the addresses used by the original binary.
Adjusted local declaration order as a matching lever, not just as source style, to influence MWCC address materialization order.
Separated a matching improvement from low-risk header cleanup; the header edits clarified prototypes without changing generated code.
Verified the change with python configure.py && ninja and a decomp.dev report showing no other lbaudio_ax regressions.
decomp_lessons: If a field access into an aggregate lands exactly on separately named symbols, try direct extern references; it can produce assembly closer to the original than an artificial struct-field path.
Local variable declaration order can affect MWCC register choice and address-load ordering, especially when multiple global addresses are materialized near a function prologue.
Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with explicit void signatures is worthwhile when the implementation already proves the ABI; it improves header truthfulness even when codegen is unchanged.
A fuzzy-match gain is useful even when not 100%; this PR documents the remaining mismatch so future work can focus on MWCC register cascade behavior instead of redoing solved symbol-address work.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions remained address-based, e.g. fn_80027488 and fn_800195FC, rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
Unidentified global labels remained lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984; the PR did not invent semantic names for the arrays.
lb library functions retained the project’s address-derived lb_800xxxxx naming pattern.
Header prototypes prefer explicit void return/argument forms over UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS when the real signature is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For fn_80027488, direct extern references to lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984 improved matching over lbl_80433710.x194 and lbl_80433710.x274 struct-field accesses.
Declaring b before a was used to make MWCC materialize lbl_80433984 before lbl_804338A4, matching the original address order more closely.
The remaining documented mismatch was one extra prologue/register-cascade difference: MWCC produced lis r3/lis r3 for two HA halves while the original used lis r4/lis r3.
No 100% matches were achieved; fn_80027488 improved from 89.55% to 94.86% fuzzy.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported one improvement in an unmatched item for GALE01: fn_80027488 in main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax improved from 89.55% to 94.86%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching fn_80027488 or lbaudio_ax.c to see whether the remaining lis register-cascade mismatch was solved.
Search for other uses of lbl_80433710 fields that overlap standalone lbl_ symbols and may benefit from direct extern references.
Search headers for remaining UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS prototypes whose C definitions already establish void signatures.
Investigate MWCC local declaration order effects on address materialization and register allocation in similar unmatched lb functions.
